files.raw_diff:
diff --git a/app/api/routes/pipeline.py b/app/api/routes/pipeline.py
index a1b2c3d..e5f6g7h 100644
--- a/app/api/routes/pipeline.py
+++ b/app/api/routes/pipeline.py
@@ -23,10 +23,14 @@
 
 @router.post("/run", status_code=202)
 async def start_run(
     body: PipelineRunRequest,
     background_tasks: BackgroundTasks,
 ) -> dict:
     """
     Start a new pipeline run in the background.
     """
+    # Input Validation Guard
+    if not body.diff_text or len(body.diff_text.strip()) < 10:
+        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="diff_text must be at least 10 characters long.")
+        
     orchestrator = PipelineOrchestrator()
     # A temporary orchestration is needed to get the run_id before starting

scenarios.title: Trigger a new pipeline run and verify queued status
scenarios.description: Verify that a POST request to the pipeline run endpoint successfully initializes a run and returns a unique run_id.

steps.assertion: Response status should be 202 and contain 'run_id' and 'status': 'queued'

scenarios.expected_result: A 202 Accepted response is returned with a valid UUID run_id.

scenarios.title: Monitor pipeline run progress via SSE stream
scenarios.description: Verify that the client can connect to the SSE stream using the run_id returned from the start-run endpoint.
preconditions: A pipeline run has been successfully queued
scenarios.expected_result: The server establishes a persistent connection for event streaming.

scenarios.title: Verify newly started run appears in the reports list
scenarios.description: Check that the run initiated via the pipeline route is visible in the reports summary list.
preconditions: A pipeline run has been initiated

steps.assertion: URL should resolve to reports view
The run_id should be present in the report table

scenarios.expected_result: The UI displays the newly created run in the list of reports.

scenarios.title: Handle invalid pipeline run request payload
scenarios.description: Ensure the API returns a validation error when the POST body is malformed or missing required fields.

steps.assertion: Response status should be 422 Unprocessable Entity

scenarios.expected_result: API rejects the request with a detailed error message regarding the payload schema.

scenarios.title: Request non-existent report details
scenarios.description: Verify that requesting details for a run_id that does not exist returns a 404 error.

steps.assertion: Response status should be 404 Not Found

scenarios.expected_result: The application handles missing report files gracefully.

scenarios.title: List reports when the reports directory is empty
scenarios.description: Verify the behavior of the list_reports function when no JSON files are present in the reports directory.
preconditions: Clean the configured REPORTS_DIR of all .json files

steps.assertion: Response should be an empty list []

scenarios.expected_result: API returns an empty array with a 200 OK status.

scenarios.title: Handle corrupted JSON report files in reports directory
scenarios.description: Verify that the API does not crash and continues to return valid reports when one report file is malformed.
preconditions: One valid .json report and one corrupted (invalid syntax) .json file exist in the reports directory

steps.assertion: The list should contain the valid report and exclude the corrupted one

scenarios.expected_result: The API logs a warning for the corrupted file but successfully returns the list of valid reports.

scenarios.title: Prevent script injection through pipeline label
scenarios.description: Ensure that labels containing script tags are properly handled and do not execute in the UI.

steps.assertion: Text should be rendered literally, no alert dialog should appear

scenarios.expected_result: The script tag is rendered as plain text or sanitized, preventing XSS.

scenarios.title: Check for Path Traversal in report retrieval
scenarios.description: Verify that the API prevents access to files outside the reports directory using relative path segments.
scenarios.expected_result: The application rejects paths containing traversal patterns.

generated_specs.content:
import os
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import Page, expect

BASE_URL = os.environ.get("BASE_URL", "http://localhost:8000")

# SCENARIO_ID: 382ba846-c539-4e57-a52a-80d09992f66b
def test_trigger_new_pipeline_run_returns_queued_status(page: Page):
    """Verify that a POST request to the pipeline run endpoint successfully initializes a run."""
    api_url = f"{BASE_URL}/api/pipeline/run"
    payload = {"label": "smoke-test-run", "config": {}}
    
    response = page.request.post(api_url, data=payload)
    
    expect(response).to_be_ok() # status 200-299
    assert response.status == 202
    
    body = response.json()
    assert "run_id" in body
    assert body["status"] == "queued"

# SCENARIO_ID: 55d842e9-742d-4316-854b-94fd9e6f38c3
def test_monitor_pipeline_run_progress_via_sse_stream(page: Page):
    """Verify client can connect to the SSE stream using the run_id."""
    # First, trigger a run to get a valid run_id
    run_response = page.request.post(f"{BASE_URL}/api/pipeline/run", data={"label": "sse-test"})
    run_id = run_response.json().get("run_id")
    
    # Connect to status stream
    status_url = f"{BASE_URL}/api/pipeline/status/{run_id}"
    response = page.request.get(status_url)
    
    expect(response).to_be_ok()
    assert "text/event-stream" in response.headers.get("content-type", "")

# SCENARIO_ID: 2acee01d-02c2-461a-a47b-76dfb751eff7
def test_newly_started_run_appears_in_reports_list_ui(page: Page):
    """Check that the run initiated via API is visible in the UI reports table."""
    # Trigger a run via API
    run_response = page.request.post(f"{BASE_URL}/api/pipeline/run", data={"label": "ui-visibility-test"})
    run_id = run_response.json().get("run_id")
    
    # Navigate to UI
    page.goto(f"{BASE_URL}/reports")
    
    # Use run_id as the name for the cell locator
    report_cell = page.get_by_role("cell", name=run_id)
    expect(report_cell).to_be_visible()

# SCENARIO_ID: 799a8210-e524-435c-ae60-39f89aa5e50b
def test_handle_invalid_pipeline_run_payload_returns_422(page: Page):
    """Ensure the API returns a validation error when the POST body is malformed."""
    api_url = f"{BASE_URL}/api/pipeline/run"
    # Send invalid payload (e.g., missing required fields or wrong type)
    response = page.request.post(api_url, data={"invalid_field": "data"})
    
    assert response.status == 422

# SCENARIO_ID: a5cace81-178f-4516-af1a-71feeb297f89
def test_request_non_existent_report_returns_404(page: Page):
    """Verify that requesting details for a non-existent run_id returns 404."""
    invalid_run_id = "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"
    response = page.request.get(f"{BASE_URL}/api/reports/{invalid_run_id}")
    
    assert response.status == 404

# SCENARIO_ID: a95347e3-38fb-4c93-a497-f517d3955362
def test_list_reports_when_directory_is_empty(page: Page):
    """Verify behavior when no reports are present."""
    # Note: This test assumes an environment where reports can be cleared or isolated
    response = page.request.get(f"{BASE_URL}/api/reports")
    
    expect(response).to_be_ok()
    assert isinstance(response.json(), list)

# SCENARIO_ID: 109287a1-7b45-4c79-92fa-e52f70684857
def test_handle_corrupted_json_report_files_gracefully(page: Page):
    """Verify API returns valid reports even if some disk files are corrupted."""
    response = page.request.get(f"{BASE_URL}/api/reports")
    
    expect(response).to_be_ok()
    reports = response.json()
    # Ensure the result is a list (even if filtered)
    assert isinstance(reports, list)

# SCENARIO_ID: 7289850f-982b-4497-a149-95938b854b03
def test_prevent_script_injection_through_pipeline_label(page: Page):
    """Ensure script tags are rendered as plain text in the UI (XSS Protection)."""
    xss_payload = "<script>alert('xss')</script>"
    
    # Trigger run with malicious label
    page.request.post(f"{BASE_URL}/api/pipeline/run", data={"label": xss_payload})
    
    # Navigate to reports page
    page.goto(f"{BASE_URL}/reports")
    
    # Verify the text is rendered literally in the UI
    script_text = page.get_by_text(xss_payload)
    expect(script_text).to_be_visible()
    
    # Verification that no dialog appeared (dialogs auto-dismiss in Playwright unless handled)
    # but 'expect' visibility of literal text is the primary check for sanitization.

# SCENARIO_ID: 7190fba2-5c7d-4539-a009-440758744c34
def test_path_traversal_prevention_in_report_retrieval(page: Page):
    """Verify API prevents access to sensitive files using path traversal."""
    traversal_path = "..%2F..%2Fetc%2Fpasswd"
    response = page.request.get(f"{BASE_URL}/api/reports/{traversal_path}")
    
    # The application should reject the malformed/unsafe path with 404 or 422
    assert response.status in [404, 422]
